NOTICE AND MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT HARO, NICOLAS HARO, RAMONA HEARING SET FOR JAN-11-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NICOLAS HARO, AND, RAMONA HARO MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FIFTH AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. WHILE A CONCEALMENT CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT REQUIRE THE SAME PARTICULARITY AS A MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION, IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE ATTEMPTING TO PLEAD BOTH. THE MISREPRESENTATION CLAIMS MUST BE PLEADED WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. MOTION GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF TO SEEK EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES UNDER THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION. MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE AS DUPLICATIVE. MOTION DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING GROUNDS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JAN-29-2010 CONTINUED TO MAR-19-2010 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212, FOR DEFENDANTS TO FILE AND SERVE ORDER RE JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Off Calendar Jan-15-2010 Continued To Mar-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NICOLAS HARO, and, RAMONA HARO Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted with leave to amend as to fifth and sixth causes of action. While a concealment cause of action does not require the same particularity as a misrepresentation cause of action, it appears that plaintiffs are attempting to plead both. The misrepresentation claims must be pleaded with more particularity. Motion granted with leave to amend as to the ninth cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. Plaintiff to seek emotional distress damages under the eighth cause of action. Motion granted without leave to amend as to the eleventh cause of action for nuisance as duplicative. Motion denied as to the remaining grounds. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
